agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to refine small-scale multi-unit housing rules in R1 zones
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would amend the Burnaby Zoning Bylaw to refine regulations for small-scale multi-unit housing (SSMUH) in the R1 District. This means the city is considering changes to rules that allow things like duplexes, triplexes, and other small multi-unit homes in areas currently zoned for single-family houses. The goal is to make the rules clearer and more workable for property owners and developers. The decision has not been made yet — council will discuss and vote on the proposed bylaw at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date and consider attending or submitting comments to have your say on the proposed changes.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
4.1 
Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 20, 2025 - Bylaw No. 
14783 
TEXT AMENDMENT 
Purpose: to amend the Burnaby Zoning Bylaw to refine regulations for small-
scale multi-unit housing (SSMUH) in the R1 District

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby zoning changes for small-scale multi-unit housing in R1 District
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would change the rules for small-scale multi-unit housing (SSMUH) in Burnaby's R1 District. The R1 District is a residential zone that currently allows only single-family homes. This proposed amendment to the Burnaby Zoning Bylaw would refine the regulations for building things like duplexes, triplexes, and townhouses on these properties. The goal is to make it easier to build more housing types in single-family neighbourhoods. The decision has not been made yet — Council will consider and vote on this proposal.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date and public hearing notice — this could affect your property if you own land in the R1 District.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
4.1
Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 20, 2025 - Bylaw No. 14783
TEXT AMENDMENT
Purpose:
to amend the Burnaby Zoning Bylaw to refine regulations for small-scale multi-unit housing (SSMUH) in the R1 District

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby council to vote on amended SSMUH bylaw
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on giving second reading to an amended bylaw (Bylaw No. 14783) related to the Small-Scale Multi-Unit Housing (SSMUH) program. This proposed bylaw would change local zoning rules to allow more types of small apartment buildings, like duplexes and triplexes, in certain residential areas. The decision is not final yet — council will consider the amended version at an upcoming meeting. If approved, this could affect property owners looking to build or redevelop smaller multi-unit housing in Burnaby.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision date — this could affect your property's development options.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
Resolution No. S2025 - 013 
THAT Bylaw No. 14783 be now read a second time, AS AMENDED.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to vote on 30% lot coverage for larger lots
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed change to the R1 District Zoning Bylaw. The change would allow a maximum lot coverage of 30% for all buildings on lots larger than 567 square metres (about 6,100 square feet) that are used for 1-to-2-unit small-scale multi-unit housing. 'Lot coverage' means the percentage of your property that can be covered by buildings. This is a proposed amendment — it has not been approved yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote on this proposed bylaw amendment — it could affect your building plans if you own a large lot.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
Resolution No. S2025 - 012 
THAT Council approve further amendments to the R1 District of the 
Burnaby Zoning Bylaw, in accordance with the following: 
Under section 101.4 in Amendment Bylaw No. 14793 the Maximum Lot 
Coverage for All Buildings for 1-to-2-unit Small-Scale Multi-Unit 
developments be changed to: 
3. 30% for lots greater than 567 m².

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to vote on 40% lot coverage for medium lots
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed change to the R1 District zoning bylaw. If approved, the maximum lot coverage for all buildings on 1-to-2-unit small-scale multi-unit developments would increase to 40% for lots between 375 and 567 square metres. This means you could build a larger building footprint on your property, but the decision has not been made yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote — this could affect your building plans if you own a lot in this size range.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
Resolution No. S2025 – 011 
THAT Council approve further amendments to the R1 District of the 
Burnaby Zoning Bylaw, in accordance with the following: 
Under section 101.4 in Amendment Bylaw No. 14793 the Maximum Lot 
Coverage for All Buildings for 1-to-2-unit Small-Scale Multi-Unit 
developments be changed to: 
2. 40% for lots greater than 375 m2 and up to 567 m2.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to vote on 40% lot coverage limit for small homes
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed change to the Burnaby Zoning Bylaw that would set a maximum lot coverage of 40% for all buildings on lots of 375 square metres or less in the R1 District. This applies to 1-to-2-unit small-scale multi-unit developments. If approved, the change would limit how much of a small lot can be covered by buildings, which could affect plans for new homes or additions.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote — if passed, this rule will affect any new building or renovation on small R1 lots.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
Resolution No. S2025 - 010 
THAT Council approve further amendments to the R1 District of the 
Burnaby Zoning Bylaw, in accordance with the following: 
Under section 101.4 in Amendment Bylaw No. 14793 the Maximum Lot 
Coverage for All Buildings for 1-to-2-unit Small-Scale Multi-Unit 
developments be changed to: 
1. 40% for lots less than or equal to 375 m2.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby zoning change to allow small-scale multi-unit housing in R1 areas
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would change the rules for R1 (single-family home) zones in Burnaby. The amendment, called the R1 SSMUH (Small-Scale Multi-Unit Housing) bylaw, would allow more than one home on a single lot — like duplexes, triplexes, or laneway houses — in areas currently limited to one detached house. This is part of a provincial push to create more housing types in residential neighbourhoods. The bylaw has been given first reading (a preliminary step), but it still needs more readings and a public hearing before it becomes law. If passed, it could affect property owners who want to build extra units on their land.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date — this is your chance to speak for or against the proposed zoning change.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
4.1.1 #14783 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 20, 2025 - 
Text Amendment (R1 District - SSMUH) 
MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. S2025 - 009 
THAT Bylaw #14783 be read a first time.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: New height and density rules for Burnaby R1 small-scale housing
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on proposed zoning bylaw amendments for the R1 District that would change the rules for small-scale multi-unit housing. The proposed changes would set new maximum building heights (measured from the lower of the front or rear elevation), new lot coverage limits (how much of the lot a building can cover), and new impervious surface limits (how much of the lot can be paved or built over). The rules would vary by lot size and number of units — for example, lots with 3-4 units could cover up to 40% of the lot and be 3 storeys tall, while rowhouse lots under 280 m² could cover 55% and be 10m tall. A rear building on a small-scale multi-unit lot could not take up more than one-third of the total allowed coverage. These changes are meant to address public concerns about building bulk and to make family-friendly housing more financially feasible for builders. The amendments have not been approved yet — Council has directed the City Solicitor to prepare them for a future vote.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date — this is your chance to speak for or against the proposed R1 zoning changes.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
Resolution No. 2025 - 527 
WHEREAS significant concerns were raised by the public on the bulk, 
massing and height regulations of the R1 District Zoning; and 
WHEREAS the financial feasibility of delivering much needed family-
friendly housing has been questioned by builders of small scale multi-unit 
housing; and 
WHEREAS given Council's desire to deliver safe, affordable and 
accessible family-oriented housing; and 
THEREFORE BE IT RESOLVED THAT Council direct the City Solicitor to 
prepare further Zoning Bylaw amendments to the R1 District substantially 
in accordance with the following: 
1. The height of a building is to be measured from the lower of the 
average front or rear elevation. 
2. The maximum height of a Small-Scale Multi-Unit building with a 
sloped roof is to be 10m to the mid-point of roof, and 9.5m for a flat 
roof measured to the highest point of the building. 
3. The maximum height of a Rear Principal Building with a sloped roof 
is to be 7.5m to the mid-point of the roof, and 7.0m for a flat roof 
measured to the highest point of the building. 
4. For Small-Scale Multi-Unit lots greater than 567 m2 in area 
accommodating 1-2 units, a total coverage of 25%, a maximum 
impervious surface area of 60%, and a height of 2.5 storeys shall 
be permitted. 
5. For Small-Scale Multi-Unit lots equal to or less than 567 m2 
accommodating 1-2 units, a total coverage of 30%, a maximum 
impervious surface area of 60%, and a height of 2.5 storeys shall 
be permitted. 
6. For Small-Scale Multi-Unit lots of any size with 3-4 units a total 
coverage of 40%, a maximum impervious surface area of 60%, and 
height of 3 storeys shall be permitted. 
7. For Small-Scale Multi-Unit lots greater than 281 m2 in area with 5-6 
units, a total coverage of 45%, a maximum impervious surface area 
of 70%, and height of 3 storeys shall be permitted. 
8. For Rowhouse lots less than 280 m2, with 3 units, a total coverage 
of 55%, a maximum impervious surface area of 70%, a height of 
10m and 3 storeys shall be permitted. 
9. That a rear principle building on a Small-Scale Multi-Unit lot shall 
not exceed 33.3% (1/3) of the total permitted coverage for that lot. 
10. That the street yard be provided at a minimum of 4.0m for Small-
Scale Multi-Unit lot, and 3.0m for a rowhouse lot.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby R1 zoning bylaw amendments to go to public hearing
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to consider a revised recommendation that would send proposed zoning bylaw amendments for the R1 SSMUH District to a future public hearing. The amendments were originally adopted in a Council report dated October 14, 2025, but the General Manager of Planning and Development now wants Council to authorize holding a public hearing before any final decision is made. This means the rules are not yet in effect — the public hearing will give residents and business owners a chance to speak before Council votes.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date — you may want to attend and share your views on the proposed R1 zoning changes.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.1 
REVISED RECOMMENDATION - R1 SSMUH DISTRICT ZONING 
BYLAW AMENDMENTS 
The General Manager Planning and Development submitted a report 
seeking Council authorization to amend one of the recommendations of 
the adopted Council report entitled "Proposed Zoning Bylaw Amendments 
– R1 SSMUH District" dated October 14, 2025, to recommend that a 
Public Hearing be held at a future date.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Council to vote on withholding building permits for non-compliant R1 applications
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposal that would bring any building permit applications that do not follow the proposed new rules for the R1 District (single-family homes) to Council for a decision. If approved, Council could use a power under Section 463 of the Local Government Act to withhold or refuse those building permits. This is not yet law — it is a proposed resolution that Council will consider at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: If you are planning a building project in an R1 zone, watch for the council decision — this could affect your permit application.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
Resolution No. 2025 - 482 
THAT any building permit applications that are non-compliant with the 
proposed changes to the R1 District be brought forward to Council for a 
resolution to potentially withhold the building permit under Section 463 of 
the Local Government Act.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby R1 zoning changes limit building height and lot coverage
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposal to change the R1 District zoning rules. A proposed bylaw would limit most new houses to 3 storeys and 10 metres in height. It would also reduce how much of a lot can be covered by buildings, especially for larger lots over 748 square metres. Rear buildings (like laneway houses) would be limited to 2 storeys and 33% of the allowed lot coverage. Balconies would not be allowed to stick out into required yards. These rules are not yet in effect — Council will consider them at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date — attend or submit comments to have your say before Council votes.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
Resolution No. 2025 - 481 
THAT the staff be directed to amend the Burnaby Zoning Bylaw to affect 
the following changes for the R1 District: 
1. Maximum height of a principal building is limited to 3 storeys 
including basement. 
2. Maximum height for a principal building is limited to 10 meters 
measured from the lower of the average front or rear elevation to 
the highest point of the roof. 
3. Maximum height for a rear building is limited to 2 storeys.  
4. Rear building(s) limited to 33% of total permitted lot coverage. 
5. For lots in excess of 748 m2, total permitted lot coverage for less 
than 3 units is reduced to 25%, and a height of 2.5 storeys. 
6. For lots less than 748m2 in size, and which generate less than two 
principal dwellings, development shall be limited to a total lot 
coverage of 30%, and a height of 2.5 storeys. 
7. Balconies are not permitted to project into required yards.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby zoning changes for small-scale multi-unit housing
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on proposed changes to the R1 Small-Scale Multi-Unit Housing (SSMUH) zoning rules. These changes would adjust the zoning bylaw to address community concerns about allowing more housing types, like duplexes and triplexes, in single-family home neighbourhoods. The proposal is not yet law — Council will consider it at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date and attend to share your views on the proposed zoning changes.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.1 
PROPOSED ZONING BYLAW AMENDMENTS - R1 SSMUH DISTRICT 
The General Manager Planning and Development, submitted a report 
proposing amendments to the Burnaby Zoning Bylaw to address 
community concerns with respect to the R1 small-scale multi-unit housing 
(SSMUH) District.

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved SSMUH zoning bylaw amendments for Burnaby R1 District
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council held a public hearing and then voted to approve Bylaw No. 14783, which amends the Burnaby Zoning Bylaw to refine regulations for small-scale multi-unit housing (SSMUH) in the R1 District. This means property owners in R1 zones now have updated rules for building things like duplexes, triplexes, and townhouses on their land. The bylaw is now in effect.
minutes_briefs.action_required: Review the new SSMUH regulations to see if your R1-zoned property now allows additional housing units.
minutes_briefs.source_body: 4.1 Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 20, 2025 - Bylaw No. 14783 TEXT AMENDMENT Purpose: to amend the Burnaby Zoning Bylaw to refine regulations for small-scale multi-unit housing (SSMUH) in the R1 District Applicant: City of Burnaby

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved SSMUH zoning bylaw for Burnaby's R1 District
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council held a public hearing and then voted unanimously to approve Bylaw No. 14783, which amends the city's zoning rules for small-scale multi-unit housing (SSMUH) in the R1 District. This means the city has finalized new regulations that will allow and refine rules for small multi-unit homes, like duplexes, triplexes, and townhouses, in areas previously zoned only for single-family homes. The decision is now complete and the bylaw has been adopted.
minutes_briefs.action_required: Review the new SSMUH regulations to see if your property in the R1 District is now eligible for redevelopment or rezoning.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
4.1
Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 20, 2025 - Bylaw No.
14783
TEXT AMENDMENT
Purpose: to amend the Burnaby Zoning Bylaw to refine regulations for small-
scale multi-unit housing (SSMUH) in the R1 District
Applicant: City of Burnaby
...
MOVED AND SECONDED
THAT this Public Hearing for the Text Amendment, Bylaw No. 14783, be
terminated.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY
